title: Verify watchlist persists across app restarts
description: WatchlistStore writes through a SwiftData ModelContext (context.save() on add/remove/reorder) and StockAlertsApp constructs the container with the default initializer: `ModelContainer(for: WatchedSymbol.self, PriceAlert.self)`, which should persist on-disk to Application Support. However, there is no test verifying the on-disk round-trip — all existing WatchlistStoreTests use TestHelpers.makeInMemoryContainer(). We need to confirm (and guard with a regression test) that a WatchedSymbol inserted during one app launch is still there on the next launch.
design:
Add a WatchlistStorePersistenceTests file under StockAlertsTests/ that:
1. Creates a temp directory URL for the store file (FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory.appendingPathComponent(UUID()...).
2. Builds container A with ModelConfiguration(url: tempURL), WatchlistStore A, adds symbols, saves, releases both.
3. Builds container B with the same URL and asserts WatchlistStore B sees the symbols.
4. Remove the temp file in tearDown.

Remember the ModelContext lifetime gotcha (CLAUDE.md): the test must retain the ModelContainer for the duration of each step — don't just keep the context.

No production code changes are expected unless the test reveals a real bug in StockAlertsApp.init() container construction.
acceptance_criteria:
- Manual smoke test: launch app, add AAPL + MSFT to the watchlist, quit the app (Cmd-Q), relaunch — both tickers reappear in the sidebar in the same order.
- Automated test exercising the real on-disk container at a test-scoped URL (ModelConfiguration(url:)), writing with one container instance and reading with a fresh one. Pattern should mirror the subprocess test idea but may stay in-process if a separate ModelContainer over the same .store URL is enough to simulate relaunch.
- If the test fails, fix root cause (likely a ModelConfiguration(isStoredInMemoryOnly:) or a sandbox-container URL issue); do not simply add a shim.
